1→(* Bridge adapter for control center frame logic.
     2→   Uses algebraic effects: OCaml performs effects, C handler executes them. *)
     3→
     4→(* Effect externals — called from OCaml effect handler, dispatch to C *)
     5→external effect_fire_weapon
     6→  :  int
     7→  -> int
     8→  -> int
     9→  -> int
    10→  -> int
    11→  -> int
    12→  -> int
    13→  -> int
    14→  -> unit
    15→  = "cd_ox_effect_cc_fire_weapon_bytecode" "cd_ox_effect_cc_fire_weapon"
    16→
    17→external effect_send_controlcen_fire
    18→  :  int
    19→  -> int
    20→  -> int
    21→  -> int
    22→  -> int
    23→  -> unit
    24→  = "cd_ox_effect_cc_send_controlcen_fire"
    25→
    26→external effect_p_rand : unit -> int = "cd_ox_effect_cc_p_rand"
    27→
    28→(* Effect handler shared by D1 and D2 *)
    29→let controlcen_effect_handler
    30→  : type a. a Effect.t -> ((a, _) Effect.Deep.continuation -> _) option
    31→  =
    32→  fun eff ->
    33→  match eff with
    34→  | Ox_controlcen.Fire_weapon (dx, dy, dz, px, py, pz, parent_id, make_sound) ->
    35→    Some
    36→      (fun k ->
    37→        effect_fire_weapon dx dy dz px py pz parent_id make_sound;
    38→        Effect.Deep.continue k ())
    39→  | Ox_controlcen.Send_controlcen_fire (dx, dy, dz, gun_num, obj_id) ->
    40→    Some
    41→      (fun k ->
    42→        effect_send_controlcen_fire dx dy dz gun_num obj_id;
    43→        Effect.Deep.continue k ())
    44→  | Ox_controlcen.P_Rand ->
    45→    Some
    46→      (fun k ->
    47→        let result = effect_p_rand () in
    48→        Effect.Deep.continue k result)
    49→  | _ -> None
    50→;;
    51→
    52→(* D1 bridge function *)
    53→let cd_do_controlcen_frame_d1
    54→      cc_been_hit
    55→      cc_player_seen
    56→      cc_next_fire_time
    57→      n_guns
    58→      gun_pos_flat
    59→      gun_dir_flat
    60→      frame_count
    61→      frame_time
    62→      game_mode
    63→      difficulty_level
    64→      player_flags
    65→      player_is_dead
    66→      game_time
    67→      player_time_of_death
    68→      obj_x
    69→      obj_y
    70→      obj_z
    71→      obj_segnum
    72→      console_x
    73→      console_y
    74→      console_z
    75→      believed_x
    76→      believed_y
    77→      believed_z
    78→      has_children
    79→      obj_id
    80→      player_objnum
    81→  =
    82→  try
    83→    Effect.Deep.match_with
    84→      (fun () ->
    85→         Ox_controlcen.do_controlcen_frame_d1
    86→           ~cc_been_hit
    87→           ~cc_player_seen
    88→           ~cc_next_fire_time
    89→           ~n_guns
    90→           ~gun_pos_flat
    91→           ~gun_dir_flat
    92→           ~frame_count
    93→           ~frame_time
    94→           ~game_mode
    95→           ~difficulty_level
    96→           ~player_flags
    97→           ~player_is_dead
    98→           ~game_time
    99→           ~player_time_of_death
   100→           ~obj_x
   101→           ~obj_y
   102→           ~obj_z
   103→           ~obj_segnum
   104→           ~console_x
   105→           ~console_y
   106→           ~console_z
   107→           ~believed_x
   108→           ~believed_y
   109→           ~believed_z
   110→           ~has_children
   111→           ~obj_id
   112→           ~player_objnum)
   113→      ()
   114→      { retc = (fun x -> x)
   115→      ; exnc = raise
   116→      ; effc = (fun (type a) (eff : a Effect.t) -> controlcen_effect_handler eff)
   117→      }
   118→  with exn ->
   119→    Printf.eprintf "[OX] controlcen_d1 exception: %s\n" (Exn.to_string exn);
   120→    Printf.eprintf "%s\n" (Printexc.get_backtrace ());
   121→    Printf.eprintf "[OX] n_guns=%d gun_pos_len=%d gun_dir_len=%d\n"
   122→      n_guns (Array.length gun_pos_flat) (Array.length gun_dir_flat);
   123→    [| cc_been_hit; cc_player_seen; cc_next_fire_time |]
   124→;;

   128→      cc_been_hit

   161→         ~cc_been_hit

1→#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2→"""
     3→Generate LLVM call graphs for Descent game logic files.
     4→
     5→Uses compile_commands.json to get exact flags, compiles to LLVM IR,
     6→runs opt -dot-callgraph, pipes through llvm-cxxfilt to demangle,
     7→then merges into a unified graph with porting status annotations.
     8→
     9→Usage:
    10→    python3 scripts/gen_callgraph.py [--render]
    11→
    12→Output:
    13→    callgraph/                     - per-file IR and DOT files
    14→    callgraph/unified.dot          - merged call graph with porting annotations
    15→    callgraph/unified.svg          - rendered SVG (if --render and graphviz installed)
    16→    callgraph/porting.json         - machine-readable porting status + graph data
    17→"""
    18→
    19→import json
    20→import os
    21→import re
    22→import subprocess
    23→import sys
    24→from collections import defaultdict
    25→from pathlib import Path
    26→
    27→PROJECT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
    28→BUILD_DIR = PROJECT / "build-c-ref"
    29→COMPILE_DB = BUILD_DIR / "compile_commands.json"
    30→LLVM_PREFIX = subprocess.check_output(
    31→    ["brew", "--prefix", "llvm@20"], text=True
    32→).strip()
    33→CLANG = f"{LLVM_PREFIX}/bin/clang++"
    34→OPT = f"{LLVM_PREFIX}/bin/opt"
    35→CXXFILT = f"{LLVM_PREFIX}/bin/llvm-cxxfilt"
    36→OUT_DIR = PROJECT / "callgraph"
    37→
    38→# Core game logic files we care about for the port
    39→GAME_LOGIC_FILES = [
    40→    "main_d2/ai.cpp",
    41→    "main_d2/ai2.cpp",
    42→    "main_d2/aipath.cpp",
    43→    "main_d2/physics.cpp",
    44→    "main_d2/collide.cpp",
    45→    "main_d2/fvi.cpp",
    46→    "main_d2/laser.cpp",

    50→    "main_d2/cntrlcen.cpp",
    51→    "main_d2/fireball.cpp",
    52→    "main_d2/wall.cpp",
    53→    "main_d2/lighting.cpp",
    54→    "main_d2/powerup.cpp",

    58→    "main_d2/morph.cpp",
    59→    "main_d2/switch.cpp",

    46→//@@vms_vector controlcen_gun_dirs[MAX_CONTROLCEN_GUNS];
    47→

    49→int Num_reactors=0;
    50→
    51→control_center_triggers ControlCenterTriggers;
    52→
    53→int	N_controlcen_guns;
    54→int	Control_center_been_hit;
    55→int	Control_center_player_been_seen;
    56→int	Control_center_next_fire_time;
    57→int	Control_center_present;

    59→vms_vector	Gun_pos[MAX_CONTROLCEN_GUNS], Gun_dir[MAX_CONTROLCEN_GUNS];
    60→
    61→//	-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    62→//return the position & orientation of a gun on the control center object 
    63→void calc_controlcen_gun_point(vms_vector *gun_point,vms_vector *gun_dir,object *obj,int gun_num)
    64→{
    65→#ifdef USE_OX_BRIDGE
    66→	struct reactor *r = &Reactors[obj->id];
    67→	Assert(obj->type == OBJ_CNTRLCEN);
    68→	Assert(obj->render_type==RT_POLYOBJ);
    69→	Assert(gun_num < r->n_guns);
    70→	cd_ox_calc_controlcen_gun_point(
    71→		r->gun_points[gun_num].x, r->gun_points[gun_num].y, r->gun_points[gun_num].z,
    72→		r->gun_dirs[gun_num].x, r->gun_dirs[gun_num].y, r->gun_dirs[gun_num].z,
    73→		obj->orient.rvec.x, obj->orient.rvec.y, obj->orient.rvec.z,
    74→		obj->orient.uvec.x, obj->orient.uvec.y, obj->orient.uvec.z,
    75→		obj->orient.fvec.x, obj->orient.fvec.y, obj->orient.fvec.z,
    76→		obj->pos.x, obj->pos.y, obj->pos.z,
    77→		&gun_point->x, &gun_point->y, &gun_point->z,
    78→		&gun_dir->x, &gun_dir->y, &gun_dir->z);
    79→#else
    80→	struct reactor *reactor;
    81→	vms_matrix m;
    82→
    83→	Assert(obj->type == OBJ_CNTRLCEN);
    84→	Assert(obj->render_type==RT_POLYOBJ);
    85→
    86→	reactor = &Reactors[obj->id];
    87→
    88→	Assert(gun_num < reactor->n_guns);
    89→
    90→	vm_copy_transpose_matrix(&m,&obj->orient);
    91→
    92→	vm_vec_rotate(gun_point,&reactor->gun_points[gun_num],&m);
    93→	vm_vec_add2(gun_point,&obj->pos);
    94→	vm_vec_rotate(gun_dir,&reactor->gun_dirs[gun_num],&m);
    95→#endif
    96→}
    97→
    98→//	-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    99→//	Look at control center guns, find best one to fire at *objp.
   100→//	Return best gun number (one whose direction dotted with vector to player is largest).

   383→def clean_name(name):
   384→    """Strip LLVM \\x01 prefix and C++ parameter list: '\\x01_fopen(...)' -> '_fopen'"""
   385→    name = name.lstrip("\x01")
   386→    paren = name.find("(")
   387→    return name[:paren] if paren >= 0 else name
   388→
   389→
   390→def parse_compile_commands():
   391→    with open(COMPILE_DB) as f:
   392→        entries = json.load(f)
   393→    commands = {}
   394→    for entry in entries:
   395→        rel = os.path.relpath(entry["file"], PROJECT)
   396→        if rel in GAME_LOGIC_FILES:
   397→            commands[rel] = entry
   398→    return commands
   399→

1→(* Pure game logic for control center frame AI.
     2→   do_controlcen_frame: detects player, selects gun, fires lasers with spread. *)
     3→
     4→let f1_0 = 0x10000
     5→let ndl = 5
     6→
     7→(* -- Algebraic effects for side effects -------------------------------- *)
     8→
     9→type _ Effect.t +=
    10→  | Fire_weapon : (int * int * int * int * int * int * int * int) -> unit Effect.t
    11→  | Send_controlcen_fire : (int * int * int * int * int) -> unit Effect.t
    12→  | P_Rand : int Effect.t
    13→
    14→(* Fire_weapon args: dir_x, dir_y, dir_z, pos_x, pos_y, pos_z, parent_id, make_sound *)
    15→(* Send_controlcen_fire args: dir_x, dir_y, dir_z, gun_num, obj_id *)
    16→(* P_Rand returns: int *)
    17→
    18→let make_random_vector () =
    19→  let rx = (Effect.perform P_Rand - 16384) lor 1 in
    20→  let ry = Effect.perform P_Rand - 16384 in
    21→  let rz = Effect.perform P_Rand - 16384 in
    22→  let _, v = Ox_math.vm_vec_copy_normalize_quick ~v:(rx, ry, rz) in
    23→  v
    24→
    25→(* FVI external for inline visibility check — reuses physics sim C function *)
    26→external fvi_find_vector_intersection : int array -> int array
    27→  = "cd_ox_effect_ps_find_vector_intersection"
    28→
    29→(* Inline visibility check using FVI ray cast.
    30→   Returns 2 if player visible (hit nothing or hit the player object), 0 otherwise. *)
    31→let check_player_visible ~obj_x ~obj_y ~obj_z ~obj_segnum ~console_x ~console_y
    32→    ~console_z ~obj_id ~player_objnum =
    33→  let query = [| obj_x; obj_y; obj_z; console_x; console_y; console_z;
    34→                 f1_0 / 4;      (* rad = F1_0/4 *)
    35→                 obj_id;        (* thisobjnum *)
    36→                 5;             (* FQ_TRANSWALL | FQ_CHECK_OBJS *)
    37→                 obj_segnum;    (* startseg *)
    38→                 0 |]           (* no ignore list *)
    39→  in
    40→  let result = fvi_find_vector_intersection query in
    41→  let hit_type = result.(0) in
    42→  let hit_object = result.(7) in
    43→  if hit_type = 0 (* HIT_NONE *)
    44→     || (hit_type = 2 (* HIT_OBJECT *) && hit_object = player_objnum)
    45→  then 2 else 0
    46→
    47→(* -- D1: do_controlcen_frame ------------------------------------------- *)
    48→
    49→let do_controlcen_frame_d1
    50→      ~cc_been_hit
    51→      ~cc_player_seen
    52→      ~cc_next_fire_time
    53→      ~n_guns
    54→      ~gun_pos_flat
    55→      ~gun_dir_flat
    56→      ~frame_count
    57→      ~frame_time
    58→      ~game_mode
    59→      ~difficulty_level
    60→      ~player_flags
    61→      ~player_is_dead
    62→      ~game_time
    63→      ~player_time_of_death
    64→      ~obj_x
    65→      ~obj_y
    66→      ~obj_z
    67→      ~obj_segnum
    68→      ~console_x
    69→      ~console_y
    70→      ~console_z
    71→      ~believed_x
    72→      ~believed_y
    73→      ~believed_z
    74→      ~has_children
    75→      ~obj_id
    76→      ~player_objnum
    77→  =
    78→  let player_flags_cloaked = 0x800 in
    79→  let cc_been_hit = ref cc_been_hit in
    80→  let cc_player_seen = ref cc_player_seen in
    81→  let cc_next_fire_time = ref cc_next_fire_time in
    82→  if not (!cc_been_hit <> 0 || !cc_player_seen <> 0)
    83→  then (
    84→    (* Detection phase: every 8 frames *)
    85→    if frame_count mod 8 = 0
    86→    then
    87→      if not (has_children <> 0)
    88→      then () (* isolated segment, bail *)
    89→      else (
    90→        let vtpx = console_x - obj_x in
    91→        let vtpy = console_y - obj_y in
    92→        let vtpz = console_z - obj_z in
    93→        let dist, (nx, ny, nz) =
    94→          Ox_math.vm_vec_copy_normalize_quick ~v:(vtpx, vtpy, vtpz)
    95→        in
    96→        if dist < f1_0 * 200
    97→        then (
    98→          let vis =
    99→            check_player_visible ~obj_x ~obj_y ~obj_z ~obj_segnum ~console_x
   100→              ~console_y ~console_z ~obj_id ~player_objnum
   101→          in
   102→          cc_player_seen := vis;
   103→          cc_next_fire_time := 0));
   104→    [| !cc_been_hit; !cc_player_seen; !cc_next_fire_time |])
   105→  else if
   106→    !cc_next_fire_time < 0
   107→    && not (player_is_dead <> 0 && game_time > player_time_of_death + (f1_0 * 2))
   108→  then (
   109→    (* Fire phase *)
   110→    let target_x, target_y, target_z =
   111→      if player_flags land player_flags_cloaked <> 0
   112→      then believed_x, believed_y, believed_z
   113→      else console_x, console_y, console_z
   114→    in
   115→    (* calc_best_gun inline via packed array *)
   116→    let packed_len = 1 + (6 * n_guns) + 3 in
   117→    let packed = Array.create ~len:packed_len 0 in
   118→    packed.(0) <- n_guns;
   119→    for i = 0 to n_guns - 1 do
   120→      packed.(1 + (3 * i)) <- gun_pos_flat.(3 * i);
   121→      packed.(1 + (3 * i) + 1) <- gun_pos_flat.((3 * i) + 1);
   122→      packed.(1 + (3 * i) + 2) <- gun_pos_flat.((3 * i) + 2)
   123→    done;
   124→    let dir_off = 1 + (3 * n_guns) in
   125→    for i = 0 to n_guns - 1 do
   126→      packed.(dir_off + (3 * i)) <- gun_dir_flat.(3 * i);
   127→      packed.(dir_off + (3 * i) + 1) <- gun_dir_flat.((3 * i) + 1);
   128→      packed.(dir_off + (3 * i) + 2) <- gun_dir_flat.((3 * i) + 2)
   129→    done;
   130→    let obj_off = 1 + (6 * n_guns) in
   131→    packed.(obj_off) <- target_x;
   132→    packed.(obj_off + 1) <- target_y;
   133→    packed.(obj_off + 2) <- target_z;
   134→    let best_gun_num = Ox_collide.calc_best_gun packed in
   135→    if best_gun_num <> -1
   136→    then (
   137→      let gun_px = gun_pos_flat.(3 * best_gun_num) in
   138→      let gun_py = gun_pos_flat.((3 * best_gun_num) + 1) in
   139→      let gun_pz = gun_pos_flat.((3 * best_gun_num) + 2) in
   140→      let vtgx = target_x - gun_px in
   141→      let vtgy = target_y - gun_py in
   142→      let vtgz = target_z - gun_pz in
   143→      let dist, (nx, ny, nz) =
   144→        Ox_math.vm_vec_copy_normalize_quick ~v:(vtgx, vtgy, vtgz)
   145→      in
   146→      if dist > f1_0 * 300
   147→      then (
   148→        cc_been_hit := 0;
   149→        cc_player_seen := 0;
   150→        [| !cc_been_hit; !cc_player_seen; !cc_next_fire_time |])

     9→(* init_thief_for_level: Initialize stolen items array for a new level.
    10→   D2 only. Returns [| stolen_items[0..9]; stolen_item_index |] *)
    11→let max_segments = 900
    12→let max_sides_per_segment = 6
    13→
    14→(* segment_is_reachable: Check if a segment side is passable for BFS reachability.
    15→   Ported from main_d2/escort.cpp segment_is_reachable().
    16→   Called with objp=NULL context, so uses buddy ailp_mode and player_flags. *)
    17→let segment_is_reachable ~curseg ~sidenum ~buddy_ailp_mode ~player_flags =
    18→  let seg_data = Effect.perform (Ox_gameseg.Fetch_segment_data curseg) in
    19→  let child = seg_data.(sidenum) in
    20→  if child <= -1 then 0
    21→  else
    22→    let wall_data = Effect.perform (Ox_aipath.Fetch_wall_data (curseg, sidenum)) in
    23→    let wall_num = wall_data.(0) in
    24→    if wall_num = -1 then 1
    25→    else
    26→      Ox_ai.ai_door_is_openable_d2
    27→        ~is_child:true
    28→        ~is_console_object:false
    29→        ~wall_num
    30→        ~wall_type:wall_data.(1)
    31→        ~wall_keys:wall_data.(2)
    32→        ~wall_flags:wall_data.(3)
    33→        ~wall_state:wall_data.(4)
    34→        ~wall_clip_num:wall_data.(5)
    35→        ~wall_controlling_trigger:wall_data.(6)
    36→        ~wallanim_flags:wall_data.(7)
    37→        ~objp_is_null:true
    38→        ~is_companion:false
    39→        ~robot_id:(-1)
    40→        ~ai_behavior:0
    41→        ~player_flags
    42→        ~ailp_mode:buddy_ailp_mode
    43→;;
    44→
    45→(* create_bfs_list: Create a breadth-first list of reachable segments.
    46→   Ported from main_d2/escort.cpp create_bfs_list().
    47→   Returns int array: element 0 = length, elements 1..length = segment numbers. *)
    48→let create_bfs_list ~start_seg ~max_segs ~buddy_ailp_mode ~player_flags =
    49→  let visited = Array.create ~len:max_segments 0 in
    50→  let bfs_list = Array.create ~len:max_segs 0 in
    51→  let head = ref 0 in
    52→  let tail = ref 0 in
    53→  bfs_list.(!head) <- start_seg;
    54→  head := !head + 1;
    55→  visited.(start_seg) <- 1;
    56→  let break_outer = ref false in
    57→  while !head <> !tail && !head < max_segs && not !break_outer do
    58→    let curseg = bfs_list.(!tail) in
    59→    tail := !tail + 1;
    60→    let i = ref 0 in
    61→    while !i < max_sides_per_segment && not !break_outer do
    62→      let seg_data = Effect.perform (Ox_gameseg.Fetch_segment_data curseg) in
    63→      let connected_seg = seg_data.(!i) in
    64→      if connected_seg > -1 && connected_seg < max_segments && visited.(connected_seg) = 0 then begin
    65→        if segment_is_reachable ~curseg ~sidenum:!i ~buddy_ailp_mode ~player_flags <> 0 then begin
    66→          bfs_list.(!head) <- connected_seg;
    67→          head := !head + 1;
    68→          if !head >= max_segs then
    69→            break_outer := true
    70→          else
    71→            visited.(connected_seg) <- 1
    72→        end
    73→      end;
    74→      i := !i + 1
    75→    done
    76→  done;
    77→  let result = Array.create ~len:(!head + 1) 0 in
    78→  result.(0) <- !head;
    79→  Array.blit ~src:bfs_list ~src_pos:0 ~dst:result ~dst_pos:1 ~len:!head;
    80→  result
    81→;;
    82→
    83→let init_thief_for_level ~game_mode =
    84→  let items = Array.create ~len:max_stolen_items 255 in
    85→  (* Assert MAX_STOLEN_ITEMS >= 3*2 — always true since 10 >= 6 *)
    86→  if game_mode land gm_multi = 0 then
    87→    for i = 0 to 2 do
    88→      items.(2 * i) <- pow_shield_boost;
    89→      items.((2 * i) + 1) <- pow_energy
    90→    done;
    91→  let result = Array.create ~len:(max_stolen_items + 1) 0 in
    92→  Array.blit ~src:items ~src_pos:0 ~dst:result ~dst_pos:0 ~len:max_stolen_items;
    93→  result.(max_stolen_items) <- 0;  (* Stolen_item_index = 0 *)
    94→  result
    95→;;
    96→

    19→  let rx = (Effect.perform P_Rand - 16384) lor 1 in

    21→  let rz = Effect.perform P_Rand - 16384 in
    22→  let _, v = Ox_math.vm_vec_copy_normalize_quick ~v:(rx, ry, rz) in

    29→(* Inline visibility check using FVI ray cast.

    31→let check_player_visible ~obj_x ~obj_y ~obj_z ~obj_segnum ~console_x ~console_y
    32→    ~console_z ~obj_id ~player_objnum =
    33→  let query = [| obj_x; obj_y; obj_z; console_x; console_y; console_z;
    34→                 f1_0 / 4;      (* rad = F1_0/4 *)
    35→                 obj_id;        (* thisobjnum *)
    36→                 5;             (* FQ_TRANSWALL | FQ_CHECK_OBJS *)
    37→                 obj_segnum;    (* startseg *)
    38→                 0 |]           (* no ignore list *)
    39→  in
    40→  let result = fvi_find_vector_intersection query in
    41→  let hit_type = result.(0) in
    42→  let hit_object = result.(7) in
    43→  if hit_type = 0 (* HIT_NONE *)
    44→     || (hit_type = 2 (* HIT_OBJECT *) && hit_object = player_objnum)
    45→  then 2 else 0
    46→
    47→(* -- D1: do_controlcen_frame ------------------------------------------- *)
    48→
    49→let do_controlcen_frame_d1
    50→      ~cc_been_hit
    51→      ~cc_player_seen
    52→      ~cc_next_fire_time

    54→      ~gun_pos_flat
    55→      ~gun_dir_flat

    61→      ~player_is_dead
    62→      ~game_time
    63→      ~player_time_of_death
    64→      ~obj_x
    65→      ~obj_y
    66→      ~obj_z
    67→      ~obj_segnum
    68→      ~console_x
    69→      ~console_y

    71→      ~believed_x
    72→      ~believed_y
    73→      ~believed_z
    74→      ~has_children

    80→  let cc_player_seen = ref cc_player_seen in
    81→  let cc_next_fire_time = ref cc_next_fire_time in

   349→              vec_z := nz2;
   350→              if game_mode land 1 <> 0
   351→              then
   352→                Effect.perform
   353→                  (Send_controlcen_fire (!vec_x, !vec_y, !vec_z, best_gun_num, obj_id));
   354→              Effect.perform
   355→                (Fire_weapon (!vec_x, !vec_y, !vec_z, gun_px, gun_py, gun_pz, obj_id, 0));
   356→              count := !count + 1)
   357→            else keep_firing := false
   358→          done;
   359→          let delta_fire_time = ref ((ndl - difficulty_level) * f1_0 / 4) in
   360→          if difficulty_level = 0 then delta_fire_time := !delta_fire_time + (f1_0 / 2);
   361→          if game_mode land 1 <> 0 then delta_fire_time := !delta_fire_time * 2;
   362→          cc_next_fire_time := !delta_fire_time;
   363→          [| !cc_been_hit; !cc_player_seen; !cc_next_fire_time; !last_vis_check |]))
   364→      else [| !cc_been_hit; !cc_player_seen; !cc_next_fire_time; !last_vis_check |])
   365→    else (
   366→      cc_next_fire_time := !cc_next_fire_time - frame_time;
   367→      [| !cc_been_hit; !cc_player_seen; !cc_next_fire_time; !last_vis_check |]))
   368→;;

file.content:
(* Pure game logic for control center frame AI.
   do_controlcen_frame: detects player, selects gun, fires lasers with spread. *)

let f1_0 = 0x10000
let ndl = 5

(* -- Algebraic effects for side effects -------------------------------- *)

type _ Effect.t +=
  | Fire_weapon : (int * int * int * int * int * int * int * int) -> unit Effect.t
  | Send_controlcen_fire : (int * int * int * int * int) -> unit Effect.t
  | P_Rand : int Effect.t

(* Fire_weapon args: dir_x, dir_y, dir_z, pos_x, pos_y, pos_z, parent_id, make_sound *)
(* Send_controlcen_fire args: dir_x, dir_y, dir_z, gun_num, obj_id *)
(* P_Rand returns: int *)

let make_random_vector () =
  let rx = (Effect.perform P_Rand - 16384) lor 1 in
  let ry = Effect.perform P_Rand - 16384 in
  let rz = Effect.perform P_Rand - 16384 in
  let _, v = Ox_math.vm_vec_copy_normalize_quick ~v:(rx, ry, rz) in
  v

(* FVI external for inline visibility check — reuses physics sim C function *)
external fvi_find_vector_intersection : int array -> int array
  = "cd_ox_effect_ps_find_vector_intersection"

(* Inline visibility check using FVI ray cast.
   Returns 2 if player visible (hit nothing or hit the player object), 0 otherwise. *)
let check_player_visible ~obj_x ~obj_y ~obj_z ~obj_segnum ~console_x ~console_y
    ~console_z ~obj_id ~player_objnum =
  let query = [| obj_x; obj_y; obj_z; console_x; console_y; console_z;
                 f1_0 / 4;      (* rad = F1_0/4 *)
                 obj_id;        (* thisobjnum *)
                 5;             (* FQ_TRANSWALL | FQ_CHECK_OBJS *)
                 obj_segnum;    (* startseg *)
                 0 |]           (* no ignore list *)
  in
  let result = fvi_find_vector_intersection query in
  let hit_type = result.(0) in
  let hit_object = result.(7) in
  if hit_type = 0 (* HIT_NONE *)
     || (hit_type = 2 (* HIT_OBJECT *) && hit_object = player_objnum)
  then 2 else 0

(* -- D1: do_controlcen_frame ------------------------------------------- *)

let do_controlcen_frame_d1
      ~cc_been_hit
      ~cc_player_seen
      ~cc_next_fire_time
      ~n_guns
      ~gun_pos_flat
      ~gun_dir_flat
      ~frame_count
      ~frame_time
      ~game_mode
      ~difficulty_level
      ~player_flags
      ~player_is_dead
      ~game_time
      ~player_time_of_death
      ~obj_x
      ~obj_y
      ~obj_z
      ~obj_segnum
      ~console_x
      ~console_y
      ~console_z
      ~believed_x
      ~believed_y
      ~believed_z
      ~has_children
      ~obj_id
      ~player_objnum
  =
  let player_flags_cloaked = 0x800 in
  let cc_been_hit = ref cc_been_hit in
  let cc_player_seen = ref cc_player_seen in
  let cc_next_fire_time = ref cc_next_fire_time in
  if not (!cc_been_hit <> 0 || !cc_player_seen <> 0)
  then (
    (* Detection phase: every 8 frames *)
    if frame_count mod 8 = 0
    then
      if not (has_children <> 0)
      then () (* isolated segment, bail *)
      else (
        let vtpx = console_x - obj_x in
        let vtpy = console_y - obj_y in
        let vtpz = console_z - obj_z in
        let dist, (nx, ny, nz) =
          Ox_math.vm_vec_copy_normalize_quick ~v:(vtpx, vtpy, vtpz)
        in
        if dist < f1_0 * 200
        then (
          let vis =
            check_player_visible ~obj_x ~obj_y ~obj_z ~obj_segnum ~console_x
              ~console_y ~console_z ~obj_id ~player_objnum
          in
          cc_player_seen := vis;
          cc_next_fire_time := 0));
    [| !cc_been_hit; !cc_player_seen; !cc_next_fire_time |])
  else if
    !cc_next_fire_time < 0
    && not (player_is_dead <> 0 && game_time > player_time_of_death + (f1_0 * 2))
  then (
    (* Fire phase *)
    let target_x, target_y, target_z =
      if player_flags land player_flags_cloaked <> 0
      then believed_x, believed_y, believed_z
      else console_x, console_y, console_z
    in
    (* calc_best_gun inline via packed array *)
    let packed_len = 1 + (6 * n_guns) + 3 in
    let packed = Array.create ~len:packed_len 0 in
    packed.(0) <- n_guns;
    for i = 0 to n_guns - 1 do
      packed.(1 + (3 * i)) <- gun_pos_flat.(3 * i);
      packed.(1 + (3 * i) + 1) <- gun_pos_flat.((3 * i) + 1);
      packed.(1 + (3 * i) + 2) <- gun_pos_flat.((3 * i) + 2)
    done;
    let dir_off = 1 + (3 * n_guns) in
    for i = 0 to n_guns - 1 do
      packed.(dir_off + (3 * i)) <- gun_dir_flat.(3 * i);
      packed.(dir_off + (3 * i) + 1) <- gun_dir_flat.((3 * i) + 1);
      packed.(dir_off + (3 * i) + 2) <- gun_dir_flat.((3 * i) + 2)
    done;
    let obj_off = 1 + (6 * n_guns) in
    packed.(obj_off) <- target_x;
    packed.(obj_off + 1) <- target_y;
    packed.(obj_off + 2) <- target_z;
    let best_gun_num = Ox_collide.calc_best_gun packed in
    if best_gun_num <> -1
    then (
      let gun_px = gun_pos_flat.(3 * best_gun_num) in
      let gun_py = gun_pos_flat.((3 * best_gun_num) + 1) in
      let gun_pz = gun_pos_flat.((3 * best_gun_num) + 2) in
      let vtgx = target_x - gun_px in
      let vtgy = target_y - gun_py in
      let vtgz = target_z - gun_pz in
      let dist, (nx, ny, nz) =
        Ox_math.vm_vec_copy_normalize_quick ~v:(vtgx, vtgy, vtgz)
      in
      if dist > f1_0 * 300
      then (
        cc_been_hit := 0;
        cc_player_seen := 0;
        [| !cc_been_hit; !cc_player_seen; !cc_next_fire_time |])
      else (
        let vec_x = ref nx in
        let vec_y = ref ny in
        let vec_z = ref nz in
        if game_mode land 1 <> 0
        then
          Effect.perform
            (Send_controlcen_fire (!vec_x, !vec_y, !vec_z, best_gun_num, obj_id));
        Effect.perform
          (Fire_weapon (!vec_x, !vec_y, !vec_z, gun_px, gun_py, gun_pz, obj_id, 1));
        (* 1/4 of time, fire another spread shot *)
        let prand = Effect.perform P_Rand in
        if prand < 32767 / 4
        then (
          let rx, ry, rz = make_random_vector () in
          let sx, sy, sz =
            Ox_math.vm_vec_scale_add2
              ~dest:(!vec_x, !vec_y, !vec_z)
              ~src:(rx, ry, rz)
              ~k:(f1_0 / 4)
          in
          let _, (nx2, ny2, nz2) = Ox_math.vm_vec_copy_normalize_quick ~v:(sx, sy, sz) in
          vec_x := nx2;
          vec_y := ny2;
          vec_z := nz2;
          if game_mode land 1 <> 0
          then
            Effect.perform
              (Send_controlcen_fire (!vec_x, !vec_y, !vec_z, best_gun_num, obj_id));
          Effect.perform
            (Fire_weapon (!vec_x, !vec_y, !vec_z, gun_px, gun_py, gun_pz, obj_id, 1)));
        let delta_fire_time = ref ((ndl - difficulty_level) * f1_0 / 4) in
        if game_mode land 1 <> 0 then delta_fire_time := !delta_fire_time * 2;
        cc_next_fire_time := !delta_fire_time;
        [| !cc_been_hit; !cc_player_seen; !cc_next_fire_time |]))
    else [| !cc_been_hit; !cc_player_seen; !cc_next_fire_time |])
  else (
    cc_next_fire_time := !cc_next_fire_time - frame_time;
    [| !cc_been_hit; !cc_player_seen; !cc_next_fire_time |])
;;

(* -- D2: do_controlcen_frame ------------------------------------------- *)

let do_controlcen_frame_d2
      ~cc_been_hit
      ~cc_player_seen
      ~cc_next_fire_time
      ~n_guns
      ~gun_pos_flat
      ~gun_dir_flat
      ~frame_count
      ~frame_time
      ~game_mode
      ~difficulty_level
      ~player_flags
      ~player_is_dead
      ~game_time
      ~player_time_of_death
      ~obj_x
      ~obj_y
      ~obj_z
      ~obj_segnum
      ~console_x
      ~console_y
      ~console_z
      ~believed_x
      ~believed_y
      ~believed_z
      ~has_children
      ~obj_id
      ~player_objnum
      ~current_level_num
      ~last_time_cc_vis_check
  =
  let player_flags_cloaked = 0x800 in
  let cc_been_hit = ref cc_been_hit in
  let cc_player_seen = ref cc_player_seen in
  let cc_next_fire_time = ref cc_next_fire_time in
  let last_vis_check = ref last_time_cc_vis_check in
  if not (!cc_been_hit <> 0 || !cc_player_seen <> 0)
  then (
    (* Detection phase: every 8 frames *)
    if frame_count mod 8 = 0
    then
      if not (has_children <> 0)
      then () (* isolated segment, bail *)
      else (
        let vtpx = console_x - obj_x in
        let vtpy = console_y - obj_y in
        let vtpz = console_z - obj_z in
        let dist, (nx, ny, nz) =
          Ox_math.vm_vec_copy_normalize_quick ~v:(vtpx, vtpy, vtpz)
        in
        if dist < f1_0 * 200
        then (
          let vis =
            check_player_visible ~obj_x ~obj_y ~obj_z ~obj_segnum ~console_x
              ~console_y ~console_z ~obj_id ~player_objnum
          in
          cc_player_seen := vis;
          cc_next_fire_time := 0));
    [| !cc_been_hit; !cc_player_seen; !cc_next_fire_time; !last_vis_check |])
  else (
    (* D2: periodic visibility re-check every 5 seconds *)
    if !cc_been_hit <> 0 || !cc_player_seen <> 0
    then
      if !last_vis_check + (f1_0 * 5) < game_time || !last_vis_check > game_time
      then (
        let vtpx = console_x - obj_x in
        let vtpy = console_y - obj_y in
        let vtpz = console_z - obj_z in
        let dist, (nx, ny, nz) =
          Ox_math.vm_vec_copy_normalize_quick ~v:(vtpx, vtpy, vtpz)
        in
        last_vis_check := game_time;
        if dist < f1_0 * 120
        then (
          let vis =
            check_player_visible ~obj_x ~obj_y ~obj_z ~obj_segnum ~console_x
              ~console_y ~console_z ~obj_id ~player_objnum
          in
          cc_player_seen := vis;
          if !cc_player_seen = 0 then cc_been_hit := 0));
    if
      !cc_next_fire_time < 0
      && not (player_is_dead <> 0 && game_time > player_time_of_death + (f1_0 * 2))
    then (
      (* Fire phase *)
      let target_x, target_y, target_z =
        if player_flags land player_flags_cloaked <> 0
        then believed_x, believed_y, believed_z
        else console_x, console_y, console_z
      in
      let packed_len = 1 + (6 * n_guns) + 3 in
      let packed = Array.create ~len:packed_len 0 in
      packed.(0) <- n_guns;
      for i = 0 to n_guns - 1 do
        packed.(1 + (3 * i)) <- gun_pos_flat.(3 * i);
        packed.(1 + (3 * i) + 1) <- gun_pos_flat.((3 * i) + 1);
        packed.(1 + (3 * i) + 2) <- gun_pos_flat.((3 * i) + 2)
      done;
      let dir_off = 1 + (3 * n_guns) in
      for i = 0 to n_guns - 1 do
        packed.(dir_off + (3 * i)) <- gun_dir_flat.(3 * i);
        packed.(dir_off + (3 * i) + 1) <- gun_dir_flat.((3 * i) + 1);
        packed.(dir_off + (3 * i) + 2) <- gun_dir_flat.((3 * i) + 2)
      done;
      let obj_off = 1 + (6 * n_guns) in
      packed.(obj_off) <- target_x;
      packed.(obj_off + 1) <- target_y;
      packed.(obj_off + 2) <- target_z;
      let best_gun_num = Ox_collide.calc_best_gun packed in
      if best_gun_num <> -1
      then (
        let gun_px = gun_pos_flat.(3 * best_gun_num) in
        let gun_py = gun_pos_flat.((3 * best_gun_num) + 1) in
        let gun_pz = gun_pos_flat.((3 * best_gun_num) + 2) in
        let vtgx = target_x - gun_px in
        let vtgy = target_y - gun_py in
        let vtgz = target_z - gun_pz in
        let dist, (nx, ny, nz) =
          Ox_math.vm_vec_copy_normalize_quick ~v:(vtgx, vtgy, vtgz)
        in
        if dist > f1_0 * 300
        then (
          cc_been_hit := 0;
          cc_player_seen := 0;
          [| !cc_been_hit; !cc_player_seen; !cc_next_fire_time; !last_vis_check |])
        else (
          let vec_x = ref nx in
          let vec_y = ref ny in
          let vec_z = ref nz in
          if game_mode land 1 <> 0
          then
            Effect.perform
              (Send_controlcen_fire (!vec_x, !vec_y, !vec_z, best_gun_num, obj_id));
          Effect.perform
            (Fire_weapon (!vec_x, !vec_y, !vec_z, gun_px, gun_py, gun_pz, obj_id, 1));
          (* D2: fire multiple spread shots based on level *)
          let rand_prob = f1_0 / ((abs current_level_num / 4) + 2) in
          let count = ref 0 in
          let keep_firing = ref true in
          while !keep_firing && !count < 4 do
            let prand = Effect.perform P_Rand in
            if prand > rand_prob
            then (
              let rx, ry, rz = make_random_vector () in
              let sx, sy, sz =
                Ox_math.vm_vec_scale_add2
                  ~dest:(!vec_x, !vec_y, !vec_z)
                  ~src:(rx, ry, rz)
                  ~k:(f1_0 / 6)
              in
              let _, (nx2, ny2, nz2) =
                Ox_math.vm_vec_copy_normalize_quick ~v:(sx, sy, sz)
              in
              vec_x := nx2;
              vec_y := ny2;
              vec_z := nz2;
              if game_mode land 1 <> 0
              then
                Effect.perform
                  (Send_controlcen_fire (!vec_x, !vec_y, !vec_z, best_gun_num, obj_id));
              Effect.perform
                (Fire_weapon (!vec_x, !vec_y, !vec_z, gun_px, gun_py, gun_pz, obj_id, 0));
              count := !count + 1)
            else keep_firing := false
          done;
          let delta_fire_time = ref ((ndl - difficulty_level) * f1_0 / 4) in
          if difficulty_level = 0 then delta_fire_time := !delta_fire_time + (f1_0 / 2);
          if game_mode land 1 <> 0 then delta_fire_time := !delta_fire_time * 2;
          cc_next_fire_time := !delta_fire_time;
          [| !cc_been_hit; !cc_player_seen; !cc_next_fire_time; !last_vis_check |]))
      else [| !cc_been_hit; !cc_player_seen; !cc_next_fire_time; !last_vis_check |])
    else (
      cc_next_fire_time := !cc_next_fire_time - frame_time;
      [| !cc_been_hit; !cc_player_seen; !cc_next_fire_time; !last_vis_check |]))
;;

1→(* Bridge adapter for control center frame logic.
     2→   Uses algebraic effects: OCaml performs effects, C handler executes them. *)
     3→
     4→(* Effect externals — called from OCaml effect handler, dispatch to C *)
     5→external effect_fire_weapon
     6→  :  int
     7→  -> int
     8→  -> int
     9→  -> int
    10→  -> int
    11→  -> int
    12→  -> int
    13→  -> int
    14→  -> unit
    15→  = "cd_ox_effect_cc_fire_weapon_bytecode" "cd_ox_effect_cc_fire_weapon"
    16→
    17→external effect_send_controlcen_fire
    18→  :  int
    19→  -> int
    20→  -> int
    21→  -> int
    22→  -> int
    23→  -> unit
    24→  = "cd_ox_effect_cc_send_controlcen_fire"
    25→
    26→external effect_p_rand : unit -> int = "cd_ox_effect_cc_p_rand"
    27→
    28→(* Effect handler shared by D1 and D2 *)
    29→let controlcen_effect_handler
    30→  : type a. a Effect.t -> ((a, _) Effect.Deep.continuation -> _) option
    31→  =
    32→  fun eff ->
    33→  match eff with
    34→  | Ox_controlcen.Fire_weapon (dx, dy, dz, px, py, pz, parent_id, make_sound) ->
    35→    Some
    36→      (fun k ->
    37→        effect_fire_weapon dx dy dz px py pz parent_id make_sound;
    38→        Effect.Deep.continue k ())
    39→  | Ox_controlcen.Send_controlcen_fire (dx, dy, dz, gun_num, obj_id) ->
    40→    Some
    41→      (fun k ->
    42→        effect_send_controlcen_fire dx dy dz gun_num obj_id;
    43→        Effect.Deep.continue k ())
    44→  | Ox_controlcen.P_Rand ->
    45→    Some
    46→      (fun k ->
    47→        let result = effect_p_rand () in
    48→        Effect.Deep.continue k result)
    49→  | _ -> None
    50→;;
    51→
    52→(* D1 bridge function *)
    53→let cd_do_controlcen_frame_d1
    54→      cc_been_hit
    55→      cc_player_seen
    56→      cc_next_fire_time
    57→      n_guns
    58→      gun_pos_flat
    59→      gun_dir_flat
    60→      frame_count
    61→      frame_time
    62→      game_mode
    63→      difficulty_level
    64→      player_flags
    65→      player_is_dead
    66→      game_time
    67→      player_time_of_death
    68→      obj_x
    69→      obj_y
    70→      obj_z
    71→      obj_segnum
    72→      console_x
    73→      console_y
    74→      console_z
    75→      believed_x
    76→      believed_y
    77→      believed_z
    78→      has_children
    79→      obj_id
    80→      player_objnum
    81→  =
    82→  try
    83→    Effect.Deep.match_with
    84→      (fun () ->
    85→         Ox_controlcen.do_controlcen_frame_d1
    86→           ~cc_been_hit
    87→           ~cc_player_seen
    88→           ~cc_next_fire_time
    89→           ~n_guns
    90→           ~gun_pos_flat
    91→           ~gun_dir_flat
    92→           ~frame_count
    93→           ~frame_time
    94→           ~game_mode
    95→           ~difficulty_level
    96→           ~player_flags
    97→           ~player_is_dead
    98→           ~game_time
    99→           ~player_time_of_death
   100→           ~obj_x
   101→           ~obj_y
   102→           ~obj_z
   103→           ~obj_segnum
   104→           ~console_x
   105→           ~console_y
   106→           ~console_z
   107→           ~believed_x
   108→           ~believed_y
   109→           ~believed_z
   110→           ~has_children
   111→           ~obj_id
   112→           ~player_objnum)
   113→      ()
   114→      { retc = (fun x -> x)
   115→      ; exnc = raise
   116→      ; effc = (fun (type a) (eff : a Effect.t) -> controlcen_effect_handler eff)
   117→      }
   118→  with exn ->
   119→    Printf.eprintf "[OX] controlcen_d1 exception: %s\n" (Exn.to_string exn);
   120→    Printf.eprintf "%s\n" (Printexc.get_backtrace ());
   121→    Printf.eprintf "[OX] n_guns=%d gun_pos_len=%d gun_dir_len=%d\n"
   122→      n_guns (Array.length gun_pos_flat) (Array.length gun_dir_flat);
   123→    [| cc_been_hit; cc_player_seen; cc_next_fire_time |]
   124→;;
   125→
   126→(* D2 bridge function *)
   127→let cd_do_controlcen_frame_d2
   128→      cc_been_hit
   129→      cc_player_seen
   130→      cc_next_fire_time
   131→      n_guns
   132→      gun_pos_flat
   133→      gun_dir_flat
   134→      frame_count
   135→      frame_time
   136→      game_mode
   137→      difficulty_level
   138→      player_flags
   139→      player_is_dead
   140→      game_time
   141→      player_time_of_death
   142→      obj_x
   143→      obj_y
   144→      obj_z
   145→      obj_segnum
   146→      console_x
   147→      console_y
   148→      console_z
   149→      believed_x
   150→      believed_y
   151→      believed_z
   152→      has_children
   153→      obj_id
   154→      player_objnum
   155→      current_level_num
   156→      last_time_cc_vis_check
   157→  =
   158→  Effect.Deep.match_with
   159→    (fun () ->
   160→       Ox_controlcen.do_controlcen_frame_d2
   161→         ~cc_been_hit
   162→         ~cc_player_seen
   163→         ~cc_next_fire_time
   164→         ~n_guns
   165→         ~gun_pos_flat
   166→         ~gun_dir_flat
   167→         ~frame_count
   168→         ~frame_time
   169→         ~game_mode
   170→         ~difficulty_level
   171→         ~player_flags
   172→         ~player_is_dead
   173→         ~game_time
   174→         ~player_time_of_death
   175→         ~obj_x
   176→         ~obj_y
   177→         ~obj_z
   178→         ~obj_segnum
   179→         ~console_x
   180→         ~console_y
   181→         ~console_z
   182→         ~believed_x
   183→         ~believed_y
   184→         ~believed_z
   185→         ~has_children
   186→         ~obj_id
   187→         ~player_objnum
   188→         ~current_level_num
   189→         ~last_time_cc_vis_check)
   190→    ()
   191→    { retc = (fun x -> x)
   192→    ; exnc = raise
   193→    ; effc = (fun (type a) (eff : a Effect.t) -> controlcen_effect_handler eff)
   194→    }
   195→;;
   196→
   197→let register_callbacks () =

file.content:
(* Bridge adapter for control center frame logic.
   Uses algebraic effects: OCaml performs effects, C handler executes them. *)

(* Effect externals — called from OCaml effect handler, dispatch to C *)
external effect_fire_weapon
  :  int
  -> int
  -> int
  -> int
  -> int
  -> int
  -> int
  -> int
  -> unit
  = "cd_ox_effect_cc_fire_weapon_bytecode" "cd_ox_effect_cc_fire_weapon"

external effect_send_controlcen_fire
  :  int
  -> int
  -> int
  -> int
  -> int
  -> unit
  = "cd_ox_effect_cc_send_controlcen_fire"

external effect_p_rand : unit -> int = "cd_ox_effect_cc_p_rand"

(* Effect handler shared by D1 and D2 *)
let controlcen_effect_handler
  : type a. a Effect.t -> ((a, _) Effect.Deep.continuation -> _) option
  =
  fun eff ->
  match eff with
  | Ox_controlcen.Fire_weapon (dx, dy, dz, px, py, pz, parent_id, make_sound) ->
    Some
      (fun k ->
        effect_fire_weapon dx dy dz px py pz parent_id make_sound;
        Effect.Deep.continue k ())
  | Ox_controlcen.Send_controlcen_fire (dx, dy, dz, gun_num, obj_id) ->
    Some
      (fun k ->
        effect_send_controlcen_fire dx dy dz gun_num obj_id;
        Effect.Deep.continue k ())
  | Ox_controlcen.P_Rand ->
    Some
      (fun k ->
        let result = effect_p_rand () in
        Effect.Deep.continue k result)
  | _ -> None
;;

(* D1 bridge function *)
let cd_do_controlcen_frame_d1
      cc_been_hit
      cc_player_seen
      cc_next_fire_time
      n_guns
      gun_pos_flat
      gun_dir_flat
      frame_count
      frame_time
      game_mode
      difficulty_level
      player_flags
      player_is_dead
      game_time
      player_time_of_death
      obj_x
      obj_y
      obj_z
      obj_segnum
      console_x
      console_y
      console_z
      believed_x
      believed_y
      believed_z
      has_children
      obj_id
      player_objnum
  =
  try
    Effect.Deep.match_with
      (fun () ->
         Ox_controlcen.do_controlcen_frame_d1
           ~cc_been_hit
           ~cc_player_seen
           ~cc_next_fire_time
           ~n_guns
           ~gun_pos_flat
           ~gun_dir_flat
           ~frame_count
           ~frame_time
           ~game_mode
           ~difficulty_level
           ~player_flags
           ~player_is_dead
           ~game_time
           ~player_time_of_death
           ~obj_x
           ~obj_y
           ~obj_z
           ~obj_segnum
           ~console_x
           ~console_y
           ~console_z
           ~believed_x
           ~believed_y
           ~believed_z
           ~has_children
           ~obj_id
           ~player_objnum)
      ()
      { retc = (fun x -> x)
      ; exnc = raise
      ; effc = (fun (type a) (eff : a Effect.t) -> controlcen_effect_handler eff)
      }
  with exn ->
    Printf.eprintf "[OX] controlcen_d1 exception: %s\n" (Exn.to_string exn);
    Printf.eprintf "%s\n" (Printexc.get_backtrace ());
    Printf.eprintf "[OX] n_guns=%d gun_pos_len=%d gun_dir_len=%d\n"
      n_guns (Array.length gun_pos_flat) (Array.length gun_dir_flat);
    [| cc_been_hit; cc_player_seen; cc_next_fire_time |]
;;

(* D2 bridge function *)
let cd_do_controlcen_frame_d2
      cc_been_hit
      cc_player_seen
      cc_next_fire_time
      n_guns
      gun_pos_flat
      gun_dir_flat
      frame_count
      frame_time
      game_mode
      difficulty_level
      player_flags
      player_is_dead
      game_time
      player_time_of_death
      obj_x
      obj_y
      obj_z
      obj_segnum
      console_x
      console_y
      console_z
      believed_x
      believed_y
      believed_z
      has_children
      obj_id
      player_objnum
      current_level_num
      last_time_cc_vis_check
  =
  Effect.Deep.match_with
    (fun () ->
       Ox_controlcen.do_controlcen_frame_d2
         ~cc_been_hit
         ~cc_player_seen
         ~cc_next_fire_time
         ~n_guns
         ~gun_pos_flat
         ~gun_dir_flat
         ~frame_count
         ~frame_time
         ~game_mode
         ~difficulty_level
         ~player_flags
         ~player_is_dead
         ~game_time
         ~player_time_of_death
         ~obj_x
         ~obj_y
         ~obj_z
         ~obj_segnum
         ~console_x
         ~console_y
         ~console_z
         ~believed_x
         ~believed_y
         ~believed_z
         ~has_children
         ~obj_id
         ~player_objnum
         ~current_level_num
         ~last_time_cc_vis_check)
    ()
    { retc = (fun x -> x)
    ; exnc = raise
    ; effc = (fun (type a) (eff : a Effect.t) -> controlcen_effect_handler eff)
    }
;;

let register_callbacks () =
  Callback.register "cd_do_controlcen_frame_d1" cd_do_controlcen_frame_d1;
  Callback.register "cd_do_controlcen_frame_d2" cd_do_controlcen_frame_d2
;;

content.thinking:
Now I understand the landscape. The function `init_controlcen_for_level` needs:

1. Iterate over all objects to find control center and boss objects
2. Potentially ghost out the control center (set type/render_type)
3. Calculate gun positions (already ported: `calc_controlcen_gun_point`)
4. Set shields on the control center object
5. Update global state variables

This function heavily mutates C globals and objects. The approach used for similar functions is to pass in the needed state as parameters from C, do the logic in OCaml, and return a result array that C writes back.


Now I need to check how this function is actually called from the C side to understand what parameters are available and what the callsite expects.

content.thinking:
Now I understand the pattern. For `init_controlcen_for_level`, the function:

1. Scans objects looking for control center and boss
2. If no cntrlcen, resets globals and returns
3. If boss exists and not multiplayer, ghosts the control center
4. Otherwise, computes gun points and sets shields
5. Resets global state

The approach should be:
- Pass needed data from C to OCaml (object types/ids, game state)
- OCaml does the logic and returns a result array
- C writes back the results

Let me think about what data needs to flow:


- From C I need the object array with types and ids, the highest object index, game mode, current level, reactor strength, the control center's id to look up its gun count, and the reactor gun positions and directions
- Back to C I need to return which object is the control center, whether it should be ghosted, and any gun-related updates plus state flags like whether it's been hit or seen the player
- The challenge is managing all this data flow — the standard pattern here is packing inputs into arrays on the C side, passing them to OCaml, getting back a result array, then unpacking on C
- Since `calc_controlcen_gun_point` is already ported and just does math, OCaml can call it directly once I have the reactor gun data available
- I need to figure out the simplest way to handle the side effects this function requires, starting with reading the necessary state and ending with applying the mutations back in C. I'm defining a set of effects to handle the data fetching and state mutations cleanly — fetching the initial object array, retrieving reactor gun data for each object, ghosting objects, and setting shields — rather than trying to port all the imperative C logic directly.

But I'm realizing the existing pattern for `do_controlcen_frame` is different: C passes all parameters and gets back a result array. For `init_controlcen_for_level`, there are no arguments — it reads from globals — so the bridge needs to pack those globals, call OCaml, then unpack and write the results back. Let me figure out the cleanest way to structure this, especially since some functions like `calc_controlcen_gun_point` are already ported to OCaml.

Looking at how `calc_controlcen_gun_point` is currently bridged, it takes input data, performs matrix operations and vector math, and returns the result. For `init_controlcen_for_level`, I'd need the C bridge to gather all the input state (object types, game mode, level, reactor data), pass it to OCaml, and then unpack the returned array of computed values back into the appropriate globals and objects. The challenge is handling the variable-length gun data without knowing the exact size upfront.

I'm realizing the simplest approach is to pack everything into one large array—object scan data for all ~350 objects plus reactor info—and let OCaml scan through it to find the control center and boss, then compute all the gun positions and shields in one pass. It's a bit wasteful but avoids the complexity of multiple phases and unknown object indices. The issue is that we don't know which object is the control center until we scan through them, and we need its reactor data to proceed. I could pass reactor data for all control center objects, but there's typically just one. Instead, I'm considering using effects—one to fetch gun point calculations and another to handle object mutations—though I need to verify what data the gun point calculation function actually requires from the reactor structure.

Looking at the function signature, it takes the reactor gun point and direction data along with the orientation matrix and object position, all passed from C. Given this is only called once per level, I think the simplest approach is to pass all the necessary data from the C side and return the result, similar to how the existing control center frame function works. So my plan is to scan the objects on the C side to build arrays of their types, IDs, and boss flags, then process each one accordingly.

Now I'm refining the data flow — the C side will scan for the control center and boss objects, gather the reactor data including gun points, directions, orientation, and position, then call into OCaml with everything packed together and get back the action to take along with the gun arrays and shield settings. file.content:

(* ── calc_controlcen_gun_point (shared D1/D2) ────────── *)

(* Pure linear algebra: transpose orient, rotate gun local pos/dir, add obj pos.
   Input: gun_local_pos(3), gun_local_dir(3), obj_orient(9), obj_pos(3) = 18 values
   Output: gun_world_pos(3), gun_world_dir(3) = 6 values *)
let calc_controlcen_gun_point
      ~glpx
      ~glpy
      ~glpz
      ~gldx
      ~gldy
      ~gldz
      ~or1
      ~or2
      ~or3
      ~ou1
      ~ou2
      ~ou3
      ~of1
      ~of2
      ~of3
      ~opx
      ~opy
      ~opz
  =
  let (tr1, tr2, tr3), (tu1, tu2, tu3), (tf1, tf2, tf3) =
    Ox_math.vm_transpose_matrix ~m:((or1, or2, or3), (ou1, ou2, ou3), (of1, of2, of3))
  in
  let gpx, gpy, gpz =
    Ox_math.vm_vec_rotate
      ~src:(glpx, glpy, glpz)
      ~m:((tr1, tr2, tr3), (tu1, tu2, tu3), (tf1, tf2, tf3))
  in
  let gpx, gpy, gpz = Ox_math.vm_vec_add2 ~a:(gpx, gpy, gpz) ~b:(opx, opy, opz) in
  let gdx, gdy, gdz =
    Ox_math.vm_vec_rotate
      ~src:(gldx, gldy, gldz)
      ~m:((tr1, tr2, tr3), (tu1, tu2, tu3), (tf1, tf2, tf3))

(* -- init_controlcen_for_level (shared D1/D2) ------------------------- *)

let gm_multi = 1
let gm_multi_robots = 4

(* Takes pre-scanned cc/boss objnums, game state, reactor data, and
   the cntrlcen object's orient + pos.
   Returns packed result:
     If ghost: [| 0 |]
     If normal: [| 1; n_guns; shields; gp0x; gp0y; gp0z; gd0x; gd0y; gd0z; ... |] *)
let init_controlcen_for_level
      ~boss_objnum
      ~game_mode
      ~current_level_num
      ~reactor_strength
      ~n_guns
      ~gun_points_flat
      ~gun_dirs_flat
      ~or1 ~or2 ~or3
      ~ou1 ~ou2 ~ou3
      ~of1 ~of2 ~of3
      ~opx ~opy ~opz
  =
  (* Ghost the CC if boss exists and NOT (multiplayer without multi-robots) *)
  let should_ghost =
    boss_objnum <> -1
    && not (game_mode land gm_multi <> 0
            && game_mode land gm_multi_robots = 0)
  in
  if should_ghost then
    [| 0 |]
  else (
    let result = Array.create ~len:(3 + n_guns * 6) 0 in
    result.(0) <- 1;
    result.(1) <- n_guns;
    (* Compute shields *)
    let shields =
      if reactor_strength = -1 then
        if current_level_num >= 0 then
          f1_0 * 200 + (f1_0 * 200 / 4) * current_level_num
        else
          f1_0 * 200 - current_level_num * f1_0 * 150
      else
        reactor_strength lsl 16
    in
    result.(2) <- shields;
    (* Compute world-space gun positions and directions *)
    for i = 0 to n_guns - 1 do
      let gpx, gpy, gpz, gdx, gdy, gdz =
        Ox_collide.calc_controlcen_gun_point
          ~glpx:gun_points_flat.(i * 3)
          ~glpy:gun_points_flat.(i * 3 + 1)
          ~glpz:gun_points_flat.(i * 3 + 2)
          ~gldx:gun_dirs_flat.(i * 3)
          ~gldy:gun_dirs_flat.(i * 3 + 1)
          ~gldz:gun_dirs_flat.(i * 3 + 2)
          ~or1 ~or2 ~or3
          ~ou1 ~ou2 ~ou3
          ~of1 ~of2 ~of3
          ~opx ~opy ~opz
      in
      result.(3 + i * 6) <- gpx;
      result.(3 + i * 6 + 1) <- gpy;
      result.(3 + i * 6 + 2) <- gpz;
      result.(3 + i * 6 + 3) <- gdx;
      result.(3 + i * 6 + 4) <- gdy;
      result.(3 + i * 6 + 5) <- gdz
    done;
    result)
;;

(* -- D2: do_controlcen_frame ------------------------------------------- *)

let do_controlcen_frame_d2

(* Pure game logic for control center frame AI.
   do_controlcen_frame: detects player, selects gun, fires lasers with spread. *)

let f1_0 = 0x10000
let ndl = 5

(* -- Algebraic effects for side effects -------------------------------- *)

type _ Effect.t +=
  | Fire_weapon : (int * int * int * int * int * int * int * int) -> unit Effect.t
  | Send_controlcen_fire : (int * int * int * int * int) -> unit Effect.t
  | P_Rand : int Effect.t

(* Fire_weapon args: dir_x, dir_y, dir_z, pos_x, pos_y, pos_z, parent_id, make_sound *)
(* Send_controlcen_fire args: dir_x, dir_y, dir_z, gun_num, obj_id *)
(* P_Rand returns: int *)

let make_random_vector () =
  let rx = (Effect.perform P_Rand - 16384) lor 1 in
  let ry = Effect.perform P_Rand - 16384 in
  let rz = Effect.perform P_Rand - 16384 in
  let _, v = Ox_math.vm_vec_copy_normalize_quick ~v:(rx, ry, rz) in
  v

(* FVI external for inline visibility check — reuses physics sim C function *)
external fvi_find_vector_intersection : int array -> int array
  = "cd_ox_effect_ps_find_vector_intersection"

(* Inline visibility check using FVI ray cast.
   Returns 2 if player visible (hit nothing or hit the player object), 0 otherwise. *)
let check_player_visible ~obj_x ~obj_y ~obj_z ~obj_segnum ~console_x ~console_y
    ~console_z ~obj_id ~player_objnum =
  let query = [| obj_x; obj_y; obj_z; console_x; console_y; console_z;
                 f1_0 / 4;      (* rad = F1_0/4 *)
                 obj_id;        (* thisobjnum *)
                 5;             (* FQ_TRANSWALL | FQ_CHECK_OBJS *)
                 obj_segnum;    (* startseg *)
                 0 |]           (* no ignore list *)
  in
  let result = fvi_find_vector_intersection query in
  let hit_type = result.(0) in
  let hit_object = result.(7) in
  if hit_type = 0 (* HIT_NONE *)
     || (hit_type = 2 (* HIT_OBJECT *) && hit_object = player_objnum)
  then 2 else 0

(* -- D1: do_controlcen_frame ------------------------------------------- *)

let do_controlcen_frame_d1
      ~cc_been_hit
      ~cc_player_seen
      ~cc_next_fire_time
      ~n_guns
      ~gun_pos_flat
      ~gun_dir_flat
      ~frame_count
      ~frame_time
      ~game_mode
      ~difficulty_level
      ~player_flags
      ~player_is_dead
      ~game_time
      ~player_time_of_death
      ~obj_x
      ~obj_y
      ~obj_z
      ~obj_segnum
      ~console_x
      ~console_y
      ~console_z
      ~believed_x
      ~believed_y
      ~believed_z
      ~has_children
      ~obj_id
      ~player_objnum
  =
  let player_flags_cloaked = 0x800 in
  let cc_been_hit = ref cc_been_hit in
  let cc_player_seen = ref cc_player_seen in
  let cc_next_fire_time = ref cc_next_fire_time in
  if not (!cc_been_hit <> 0 || !cc_player_seen <> 0)
  then (
    (* Detection phase: every 8 frames *)
    if frame_count mod 8 = 0
    then
      if not (has_children <> 0)
      then () (* isolated segment, bail *)
      else (
        let vtpx = console_x - obj_x in
        let vtpy = console_y - obj_y in
        let vtpz = console_z - obj_z in
        let dist, (nx, ny, nz) =
          Ox_math.vm_vec_copy_normalize_quick ~v:(vtpx, vtpy, vtpz)
        in
        if dist < f1_0 * 200
        then (
          let vis =
            check_player_visible ~obj_x ~obj_y ~obj_z ~obj_segnum ~console_x
              ~console_y ~console_z ~obj_id ~player_objnum
          in
          cc_player_seen := vis;
          cc_next_fire_time := 0));
    [| !cc_been_hit; !cc_player_seen; !cc_next_fire_time |])
  else if
    !cc_next_fire_time < 0
    && not (player_is_dead <> 0 && game_time > player_time_of_death + (f1_0 * 2))
  then (
    (* Fire phase *)
    let target_x, target_y, target_z =
      if player_flags land player_flags_cloaked <> 0
      then believed_x, believed_y, believed_z
      else console_x, console_y, console_z
    in
    (* calc_best_gun inline via packed array *)
    let packed_len = 1 + (6 * n_guns) + 3 in
    let packed = Array.create ~len:packed_len 0 in
    packed.(0) <- n_guns;
    for i = 0 to n_guns - 1 do
      packed.(1 + (3 * i)) <- gun_pos_flat.(3 * i);
      packed.(1 + (3 * i) + 1) <- gun_pos_flat.((3 * i) + 1);
      packed.(1 + (3 * i) + 2) <- gun_pos_flat.((3 * i) + 2)
    done;
    let dir_off = 1 + (3 * n_guns) in
    for i = 0 to n_guns - 1 do
      packed.(dir_off + (3 * i)) <- gun_dir_flat.(3 * i);
      packed.(dir_off + (3 * i) + 1) <- gun_dir_flat.((3 * i) + 1);
      packed.(dir_off + (3 * i) + 2) <- gun_dir_flat.((3 * i) + 2)
    done;
    let obj_off = 1 + (6 * n_guns) in
    packed.(obj_off) <- target_x;
    packed.(obj_off + 1) <- target_y;
    packed.(obj_off + 2) <- target_z;
    let best_gun_num = Ox_collide.calc_best_gun packed in
    if best_gun_num <> -1
    then (
      let gun_px = gun_pos_flat.(3 * best_gun_num) in
      let gun_py = gun_pos_flat.((3 * best_gun_num) + 1) in
      let gun_pz = gun_pos_flat.((3 * best_gun_num) + 2) in
      let vtgx = target_x - gun_px in
      let vtgy = target_y - gun_py in
      let vtgz = target_z - gun_pz in
      let dist, (nx, ny, nz) =
        Ox_math.vm_vec_copy_normalize_quick ~v:(vtgx, vtgy, vtgz)
      in
      if dist > f1_0 * 300
      then (
        cc_been_hit := 0;
        cc_player_seen := 0;
        [| !cc_been_hit; !cc_player_seen; !cc_next_fire_time |])
      else (
        let vec_x = ref nx in
        let vec_y = ref ny in
        let vec_z = ref nz in
        if game_mode land 1 <> 0
        then
          Effect.perform
            (Send_controlcen_fire (!vec_x, !vec_y, !vec_z, best_gun_num, obj_id));
        Effect.perform
          (Fire_weapon (!vec_x, !vec_y, !vec_z, gun_px, gun_py, gun_pz, obj_id, 1));
        (* 1/4 of time, fire another spread shot *)
        let prand = Effect.perform P_Rand in
        if prand < 32767 / 4
        then (
          let rx, ry, rz = make_random_vector () in
          let sx, sy, sz =
            Ox_math.vm_vec_scale_add2
              ~dest:(!vec_x, !vec_y, !vec_z)
              ~src:(rx, ry, rz)
              ~k:(f1_0 / 4)
          in
          let _, (nx2, ny2, nz2) = Ox_math.vm_vec_copy_normalize_quick ~v:(sx, sy, sz) in
          vec_x := nx2;
          vec_y := ny2;
          vec_z := nz2;
          if game_mode land 1 <> 0
          then
            Effect.perform
              (Send_controlcen_fire (!vec_x, !vec_y, !vec_z, best_gun_num, obj_id));
          Effect.perform
            (Fire_weapon (!vec_x, !vec_y, !vec_z, gun_px, gun_py, gun_pz, obj_id, 1)));
        let delta_fire_time = ref ((ndl - difficulty_level) * f1_0 / 4) in
        if game_mode land 1 <> 0 then delta_fire_time := !delta_fire_time * 2;
        cc_next_fire_time := !delta_fire_time;
        [| !cc_been_hit; !cc_player_seen; !cc_next_fire_time |]))
    else [| !cc_been_hit; !cc_player_seen; !cc_next_fire_time |])
  else (
    cc_next_fire_time := !cc_next_fire_time - frame_time;
    [| !cc_been_hit; !cc_player_seen; !cc_next_fire_time |])
;;

(* -- D2: do_controlcen_frame ------------------------------------------- *)

let do_controlcen_frame_d2
      ~cc_been_hit
      ~cc_player_seen
      ~cc_next_fire_time
      ~n_guns
      ~gun_pos_flat
      ~gun_dir_flat
      ~frame_count
      ~frame_time
      ~game_mode
      ~difficulty_level
      ~player_flags
      ~player_is_dead
      ~game_time
      ~player_time_of_death
      ~obj_x
      ~obj_y
      ~obj_z
      ~obj_segnum
      ~console_x
      ~console_y
      ~console_z
      ~believed_x
      ~believed_y
      ~believed_z
      ~has_children
      ~obj_id
      ~player_objnum
      ~current_level_num
      ~last_time_cc_vis_check
  =
  let player_flags_cloaked = 0x800 in
  let cc_been_hit = ref cc_been_hit in
  let cc_player_seen = ref cc_player_seen in
  let cc_next_fire_time = ref cc_next_fire_time in
  let last_vis_check = ref last_time_cc_vis_check in
  if not (!cc_been_hit <> 0 || !cc_player_seen <> 0)
  then (
    (* Detection phase: every 8 frames *)
    if frame_count mod 8 = 0
    then
      if not (has_children <> 0)
      then () (* isolated segment, bail *)
      else (
        let vtpx = console_x - obj_x in
        let vtpy = console_y - obj_y in
        let vtpz = console_z - obj_z in
        let dist, (nx, ny, nz) =
          Ox_math.vm_vec_copy_normalize_quick ~v:(vtpx, vtpy, vtpz)
        in
        if dist < f1_0 * 200
        then (
          let vis =
            check_player_visible ~obj_x ~obj_y ~obj_z ~obj_segnum ~console_x
              ~console_y ~console_z ~obj_id ~player_objnum
          in
          cc_player_seen := vis;
          cc_next_fire_time := 0));
    [| !cc_been_hit; !cc_player_seen; !cc_next_fire_time; !last_vis_check |])
  else (
    (* D2: periodic visibility re-check every 5 seconds *)
    if !cc_been_hit <> 0 || !cc_player_seen <> 0
    then
      if !last_vis_check + (f1_0 * 5) < game_time || !last_vis_check > game_time
      then (
        let vtpx = console_x - obj_x in
        let vtpy = console_y - obj_y in
        let vtpz = console_z - obj_z in
        let dist, (nx, ny, nz) =
          Ox_math.vm_vec_copy_normalize_quick ~v:(vtpx, vtpy, vtpz)
        in
        last_vis_check := game_time;
        if dist < f1_0 * 120
        then (
          let vis =
            check_player_visible ~obj_x ~obj_y ~obj_z ~obj_segnum ~console_x
              ~console_y ~console_z ~obj_id ~player_objnum
          in
          cc_player_seen := vis;
          if !cc_player_seen = 0 then cc_been_hit := 0));
    if
      !cc_next_fire_time < 0
      && not (player_is_dead <> 0 && game_time > player_time_of_death + (f1_0 * 2))
    then (
      (* Fire phase *)
      let target_x, target_y, target_z =
        if player_flags land player_flags_cloaked <> 0
        then believed_x, believed_y, believed_z
        else console_x, console_y, console_z
      in
      let packed_len = 1 + (6 * n_guns) + 3 in
      let packed = Array.create ~len:packed_len 0 in
      packed.(0) <- n_guns;
      for i = 0 to n_guns - 1 do
        packed.(1 + (3 * i)) <- gun_pos_flat.(3 * i);
        packed.(1 + (3 * i) + 1) <- gun_pos_flat.((3 * i) + 1);
        packed.(1 + (3 * i) + 2) <- gun_pos_flat.((3 * i) + 2)
      done;
      let dir_off = 1 + (3 * n_guns) in
      for i = 0 to n_guns - 1 do
        packed.(dir_off + (3 * i)) <- gun_dir_flat.(3 * i);
        packed.(dir_off + (3 * i) + 1) <- gun_dir_flat.((3 * i) + 1);
        packed.(dir_off + (3 * i) + 2) <- gun_dir_flat.((3 * i) + 2)
      done;
      let obj_off = 1 + (6 * n_guns) in
      packed.(obj_off) <- target_x;
      packed.(obj_off + 1) <- target_y;
      packed.(obj_off + 2) <- target_z;
      let best_gun_num = Ox_collide.calc_best_gun packed in
      if best_gun_num <> -1
      then (
        let gun_px = gun_pos_flat.(3 * best_gun_num) in
        let gun_py = gun_pos_flat.((3 * best_gun_num) + 1) in
        let gun_pz = gun_pos_flat.((3 * best_gun_num) + 2) in
        let vtgx = target_x - gun_px in
        let vtgy = target_y - gun_py in
        let vtgz = target_z - gun_pz in
        let dist, (nx, ny, nz) =
          Ox_math.vm_vec_copy_normalize_quick ~v:(vtgx, vtgy, vtgz)
        in
        if dist > f1_0 * 300
        then (
          cc_been_hit := 0;
          cc_player_seen := 0;
          [| !cc_been_hit; !cc_player_seen; !cc_next_fire_time; !last_vis_check |])
        else (
          let vec_x = ref nx in
          let vec_y = ref ny in
          let vec_z = ref nz in
          if game_mode land 1 <> 0
          then
            Effect.perform
              (Send_controlcen_fire (!vec_x, !vec_y, !vec_z, best_gun_num, obj_id));
          Effect.perform
            (Fire_weapon (!vec_x, !vec_y, !vec_z, gun_px, gun_py, gun_pz, obj_id, 1));
          (* D2: fire multiple spread shots based on level *)
          let rand_prob = f1_0 / ((abs current_level_num / 4) + 2) in
          let count = ref 0 in
          let keep_firing = ref true in
          while !keep_firing && !count < 4 do
            let prand = Effect.perform P_Rand in
            if prand > rand_prob
            then (
              let rx, ry, rz = make_random_vector () in
              let sx, sy, sz =
                Ox_math.vm_vec_scale_add2
                  ~dest:(!vec_x, !vec_y, !vec_z)
                  ~src:(rx, ry, rz)
                  ~k:(f1_0 / 6)
              in
              let _, (nx2, ny2, nz2) =
                Ox_math.vm_vec_copy_normalize_quick ~v:(sx, sy, sz)
              in
              vec_x := nx2;
              vec_y := ny2;
              vec_z := nz2;
              if game_mode land 1 <> 0
              then
                Effect.perform
                  (Send_controlcen_fire (!vec_x, !vec_y, !vec_z, best_gun_num, obj_id));
              Effect.perform
                (Fire_weapon (!vec_x, !vec_y, !vec_z, gun_px, gun_py, gun_pz, obj_id, 0));
              count := !count + 1)
            else keep_firing := false
          done;
          let delta_fire_time = ref ((ndl - difficulty_level) * f1_0 / 4) in
          if difficulty_level = 0 then delta_fire_time := !delta_fire_time + (f1_0 / 2);
          if game_mode land 1 <> 0 then delta_fire_time := !delta_fire_time * 2;
          cc_next_fire_time := !delta_fire_time;
          [| !cc_been_hit; !cc_player_seen; !cc_next_fire_time; !last_vis_check |]))
      else [| !cc_been_hit; !cc_player_seen; !cc_next_fire_time; !last_vis_check |])
    else (
      cc_next_fire_time := !cc_next_fire_time - frame_time;
      [| !cc_been_hit; !cc_player_seen; !cc_next_fire_time; !last_vis_check |]))
;;

(* Bridge adapter for control center frame logic.
   Uses algebraic effects: OCaml performs effects, C handler executes them. *)

(* Effect externals — called from OCaml effect handler, dispatch to C *)
external effect_fire_weapon
  :  int
  -> int
  -> int
  -> int
  -> int
  -> int
  -> int
  -> int
  -> unit
  = "cd_ox_effect_cc_fire_weapon_bytecode" "cd_ox_effect_cc_fire_weapon"

external effect_send_controlcen_fire
  :  int
  -> int
  -> int
  -> int
  -> int
  -> unit
  = "cd_ox_effect_cc_send_controlcen_fire"

external effect_p_rand : unit -> int = "cd_ox_effect_cc_p_rand"

(* Effect handler shared by D1 and D2 *)
let controlcen_effect_handler
  : type a. a Effect.t -> ((a, _) Effect.Deep.continuation -> _) option
  =
  fun eff ->
  match eff with
  | Ox_controlcen.Fire_weapon (dx, dy, dz, px, py, pz, parent_id, make_sound) ->
    Some
      (fun k ->
        effect_fire_weapon dx dy dz px py pz parent_id make_sound;
        Effect.Deep.continue k ())
  | Ox_controlcen.Send_controlcen_fire (dx, dy, dz, gun_num, obj_id) ->
    Some
      (fun k ->
        effect_send_controlcen_fire dx dy dz gun_num obj_id;
        Effect.Deep.continue k ())
  | Ox_controlcen.P_Rand ->
    Some
      (fun k ->
        let result = effect_p_rand () in
        Effect.Deep.continue k result)
  | _ -> None
;;

(* D1 bridge function *)
let cd_do_controlcen_frame_d1
      cc_been_hit
      cc_player_seen
      cc_next_fire_time
      n_guns
      gun_pos_flat
      gun_dir_flat
      frame_count
      frame_time
      game_mode
      difficulty_level
      player_flags
      player_is_dead
      game_time
      player_time_of_death
      obj_x
      obj_y
      obj_z
      obj_segnum
      console_x
      console_y
      console_z
      believed_x
      believed_y
      believed_z
      has_children
      obj_id
      player_objnum
  =
  try
    Effect.Deep.match_with
      (fun () ->
         Ox_controlcen.do_controlcen_frame_d1
           ~cc_been_hit
           ~cc_player_seen
           ~cc_next_fire_time
           ~n_guns
           ~gun_pos_flat
           ~gun_dir_flat
           ~frame_count
           ~frame_time
           ~game_mode
           ~difficulty_level
           ~player_flags
           ~player_is_dead
           ~game_time
           ~player_time_of_death
           ~obj_x
           ~obj_y
           ~obj_z
           ~obj_segnum
           ~console_x
           ~console_y
           ~console_z
           ~believed_x
           ~believed_y
           ~believed_z
           ~has_children
           ~obj_id
           ~player_objnum)
      ()
      { retc = (fun x -> x)
      ; exnc = raise
      ; effc = (fun (type a) (eff : a Effect.t) -> controlcen_effect_handler eff)
      }
  with exn ->
    Printf.eprintf "[OX] controlcen_d1 exception: %s\n" (Exn.to_string exn);
    Printf.eprintf "%s\n" (Printexc.get_backtrace ());
    Printf.eprintf "[OX] n_guns=%d gun_pos_len=%d gun_dir_len=%d\n"
      n_guns (Array.length gun_pos_flat) (Array.length gun_dir_flat);
    [| cc_been_hit; cc_player_seen; cc_next_fire_time |]
;;

(* D2 bridge function *)
let cd_do_controlcen_frame_d2
      cc_been_hit
      cc_player_seen

      n_guns
      gun_pos_flat
      gun_dir_flat
      frame_count

      game_time
      player_time_of_death
      obj_x
      obj_y
      obj_z
      obj_segnum
      console_x
      console_y
      console_z
      believed_x
      believed_y
      believed_z
      has_children
      obj_id
      player_objnum
      current_level_num
      last_time_cc_vis_check
  =
  Effect.Deep.match_with
    (fun () ->
       Ox_controlcen.do_controlcen_frame_d2
         ~cc_been_hit
         ~cc_player_seen
         ~cc_next_fire_time
         ~n_guns
         ~gun_pos_flat

         ~frame_count

         ~game_mode

         ~player_flags
         ~player_is_dead
         ~game_time

         ~obj_x

         ~console_x

         ~believed_x
         ~believed_y
         ~believed_z
         ~has_children
         ~obj_id
         ~player_objnum
         ~current_level_num
         ~last_time_cc_vis_check)
    ()
    { retc = (fun x -> x)
    ; exnc = raise
    ; effc = (fun (type a) (eff : a Effect.t) -> controlcen_effect_handler eff)
    }
;;

  Callback.register "cd_do_controlcen_frame_d1" cd_do_controlcen_frame_d1;
  Callback.register "cd_do_controlcen_frame_d2" cd_do_controlcen_frame_d2

/* init_controlcen_for_level: initialize control center for a new level.
   C pre-scans objects to find cc_objnum/boss_objnum, packs reactor data.
   result must have room for (3 + MAX_CONTROLCEN_GUNS*6) ints.
   Result[0]: 0=ghost, 1=normal.

/* Collide / damage effect callback typedefs */
typedef void (*cd_effect_increment_kills_fn)(void);

    cd_effect_send_net_robot_explode_fn send_net_robot_explode,
    cd_effect_multi_explode_robot_sub_fn multi_explode_robot_sub);

   Effect callbacks are invoked during execution via OCaml algebraic effects. */

   Called from OCaml FVI when FQ_TRANSPOINT is set.
   Args: segnum, sidenum, facenum, hit_x, hit_y, hit_z
   Returns: 1 if pixel is transparent (passable), 0 if solid */
typedef int (*cd_check_trans_wall_fn)(int segnum, int sidenum, int facenum,

int cd_ox_find_homing_object_complete_v2(const int32_t* packed, int packed_len);

/* find_homing_object v2: on-demand data via effects. */
int cd_ox_find_homing_object_v2(const int32_t* packed, int packed_len);

/* track_track_goal v2: on-demand data via effects. */

/* player_is_visible_from_object v2: on-demand data via effects. */
void cd_ox_player_is_visible_from_object_v2(const int32_t* packed, int packed_len, int32_t* out);

/* compute_vis_and_vec v2: on-demand data via effects. */
void cd_ox_compute_vis_and_vec_v2(int32_t* packed, int packed_len, int32_t* out);

/* robot_get_anim_state: look up joint positions for a gun/state combo.

    const int32_t* packed, int packed_len,

   packed layout: [anim_states..., robot_joints..., n_guns, anim_angles(30), state,
                   anim_states_len, robot_joints_len]

    const int32_t* packed, int packed_len,

/* robot_set_angles: build anim_states table and joint entries.

   out_len: total number of ints written to out_buf */
void cd_ox_robot_set_angles(
    const int32_t* packed, int packed_len,
    int32_t* out_buf, int* out_len);

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Generate LLVM call graphs for Descent game logic files.

Uses compile_commands.json to get exact flags, compiles to LLVM IR,
runs opt -dot-callgraph, pipes through llvm-cxxfilt to demangle,
then merges into a unified graph with porting status annotations.

Usage:
    python3 scripts/gen_callgraph.py [--render]

Output:
    callgraph/                     - per-file IR and DOT files
    callgraph/unified.dot          - merged call graph with porting annotations
    callgraph/unified.svg          - rendered SVG (if --render and graphviz installed)
    callgraph/porting.json         - machine-readable porting status + graph data
"""

import json
import os
import re
import subprocess
import sys
from collections import defaultdict
from pathlib import Path

PROJECT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
BUILD_DIR = PROJECT / "build-c-ref"
COMPILE_DB = BUILD_DIR / "compile_commands.json"
LLVM_PREFIX = subprocess.check_output(
    ["brew", "--prefix", "llvm@20"], text=True
).strip()
CLANG = f"{LLVM_PREFIX}/bin/clang++"
OPT = f"{LLVM_PREFIX}/bin/opt"
CXXFILT = f"{LLVM_PREFIX}/bin/llvm-cxxfilt"
OUT_DIR = PROJECT / "callgraph"

# Core game logic files we care about for the port
GAME_LOGIC_FILES = [
    "main_d2/ai.cpp",
    "main_d2/ai2.cpp",
    "main_d2/aipath.cpp",
    "main_d2/physics.cpp",
    "main_d2/collide.cpp",
    "main_d2/fvi.cpp",
    "main_d2/laser.cpp",
    "main_d2/weapon.cpp",
    "main_d2/object.cpp",
    "main_d2/gameseg.cpp",
    "main_d2/cntrlcen.cpp",
    "main_d2/fireball.cpp",
    "main_d2/wall.cpp",
    "main_d2/lighting.cpp",
    "main_d2/powerup.cpp",
    "main_d2/fuelcen.cpp",
    "main_d2/escort.cpp",
    "main_d2/robot.cpp",

    "main_d2/switch.cpp",

]
